content.text: Operators combine values into expressions that drive agent decisions: comparing tool latencies, combining boolean guard flags, and building derived configuration. Understanding precedence, short-circuit behavior, and identity versus equality prevents logic bugs in multi-step agent loops.

attrs.alt: Python operators: arithmetic, comparison, logical, bitwise, and augmented assignment with precedence

content.text: is a symbol that performs an operation on one or more operands. An
is any combination of values, variables, and operators that evaluates to a single value.
Agent control flow depends on expressions: retry while attempts remain, skip tools when budget is exhausted, route to fallback models when confidence is low. A misplaced
can send requests down the wrong branch.
— assign and test in one expression, useful in loops parsing streams
— dense expressions without parentheses can surprise readers
— empty strings and zero are falsy; explicit comparisons are clearer in agent policy code

a = [1, 2]
b = [1, 2]
c = a

a == b   # True — same values
c is a   # True — same object
a is b   # False — different list objects

# Use == for value comparison
# Use is for None, singletons, and identity checks only

def expensive_check():
    print("running expensive check")
    return True

# Second operand never evaluated if first is False
False and expensive_check()  # prints nothing

# Guard pattern for agent tools
if tool_enabled and expensive_check():
    invoke_tool()
— checking allowed tool names in a set

MAX_LATENCY_MS = 2000
attempt = 3
MAX_ATTEMPTS = 5

should_retry = (
    attempt < MAX_ATTEMPTS
    and latency_ms < MAX_LATENCY_MS
    and error_code not in FATAL_ERRORS
)

# Walrus: parse and test in a loop
while (line := log_stream.readline()):
    if "ERROR" in line:
        handle_error(line)

# Token budget check
remaining = max_tokens - used_tokens
can_continue = remaining > 0 and not stop_requested

# Chained comparison
0 <= confidence <= 1.0  # valid Python idiom

# String concatenation vs. addition trap
"gpt-" + model_version   # OK for strings
# model_version + 1      # TypeError if version is str
